solution: 
```python
class Solution:
    def minimumLength(self, s: str) -> int:
        left, right = 0, len(s) - 1
        while left < right and s[left] == s[right]:
            ch = s[left]
            while left <= right and s[left] == ch:
                left += 1
            while left <= right and s[right] == ch:
                right -= 1
        return right - left + 1
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: <The algorithm uses two pointers to find matching prefix and suffix characters and removes them by moving the pointers inward. It continues until no more matching prefix and suffix can be found. The key strength is its linear time and constant space complexity, making it very efficient for large inputs. The weakness is that it only considers the outermost matching characters and may not account for all possible removal sequences.>
